skill.name: code-quality
skill.description: Expert at TypeScript strict mode, linting, formatting, code review standards. Use when checking code quality, fixing type errors, or enforcing standards.
skill.summary: Expert at TypeScript strict mode, linting, formatting, code review standards. Use when checking code...
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, example TypeScript, and inline code as shell execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and sensitive environment access. Manual review found these are false positives, with no evidence of network exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The skill uses file-reading tools and may run local quality commands, so it is low risk and safe to publish with normal command review.

low_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Quality Checks
low_findings.description: The skill allows Bash and documents pnpm tsc --noEmit as a quality check. This is a legitimate code-quality workflow, but local shell commands should still be reviewed before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly permits Bash and names a local type-check command. The command is standard and not user-controlled, so the residual risk is limited.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Is a Non-Secret Example
low_findings.description: The process.env.NODE_ENV reference is example TypeScript for development logging. It does not read secrets, access an environment file, or send data elsewhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only environment variable shown is NODE_ENV inside a documentation example. No evidence found for secret harvesting or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick execution detections map to prose, inline code, code fences, or checklists. No executable Ruby, cryptographic function, or reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these locations are plain Markdown or TypeScript examples. The detected patterns do not indicate executable malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: TypeScript and React projects often drift into inconsistent patterns, weak typing, and noisy review cycles. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code apply strict typing, component standards, and practical review checklists.
actual_capabilities: Reviews TypeScript code for strict mode expectations and explicit typing.
Identifies common type errors and recommends focused fixes.
Applies React component and hook structure conventions.
Promotes TSDoc comments for props, hooks, and exported functions.
Uses a checklist for naming, error handling, tests, and Storybook coverage.
limitations: Focused on TypeScript and React patterns, not every programming language.
Project-specific platform rules may not match all repositories.
Quality commands depend on the package manager and scripts available locally.
It provides review guidance and examples, not a full static analysis engine.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Review changed TypeScript and React files before a teammate reviews the pull request.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Patterns
use_cases.description: Apply shared component, hook, typing, and documentation rules across a codebase.

use_cases.title: Resolve Type Errors
use_cases.description: Turn compiler messages into clear fixes with safer types and better null handling.

prompt_templates.title: Check My TypeScript
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one file or small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this TypeScript change for strict mode issues, unsafe any usage, and missing null checks. Give concise fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Review a React Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for props typing, hook order, memoization, TSDoc, and production logging issues.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Compiler Errors
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced troubleshooting with local type-check output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TypeScript errors from the project to identify root causes. Suggest minimal fixes that preserve type safety.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this pull request for type safety, component consistency, hook design, platform abstraction rules, tests, and Storybook coverage.

output_examples.input: Review a component that uses loosely typed props and logs debug data.
output_examples.output: Findings would call out missing prop documentation, unsafe typing, production console output, and a clearer typed props interface.

output_examples.input: Analyze TypeScript errors from a strict mode build.
output_examples.output: The response would group errors by cause, explain the likely type mismatch, and recommend safer annotations or type guards.

output_examples.input: Check a hook before merging a pull request.
output_examples.output: The review would cover return type shape, loading and error states, cleanup behavior, and dependency handling.

best_practices: Run type checks before requesting a final code-quality review.
Share the relevant files and compiler output for more precise recommendations.
Apply the checklist to changed code first, then expand to nearby shared patterns.
anti_patterns: Using type assertions to bypass errors without validating runtime data.
Asking for broad cleanup without defining the repository standards to preserve.
Ignoring tests, stories, and documentation while changing shared components.

faq.question: Does this skill run a full security scan?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on TypeScript, React, linting, formatting, and review quality.

faq.question: Can it fix every TypeScript error automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend fixes, but complex type design still needs project context.

faq.question: Does it require Bash access?
faq.answer: It can use Bash for local quality commands such as type checks when the user permits them.

faq.question: Is it only for React projects?
faq.answer: React guidance is included, but the TypeScript strict mode advice can help other TypeScript projects.

faq.question: Will it enforce one formatting tool?
faq.answer: No. It follows the repository's existing linting and formatting setup when available.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
